migration_policy: additive until 1.x; breaking changes require migration note, fixture update, backwards-compatibility decision, explicit reviewer approval
title: CC cram sheet (weak-area review packet)
description: A deterministic weak-area review packet built by cc_spine.cram_sheet.build_cram_sheet (PR-11c). Unlike the content-free readiness dashboard, a cram sheet DELIBERATELY carries teaching content (trap/correction/one_sentence_rule) — the committed, vetted misconception-registry explanation behind the learner's own past misses on items they already attempted. It never carries the item's choices, the isolated-key fields (correct_index / answer_key_ref / rationale_refs / distractor_misconceptions / hashes), the learner's own selected_index, or any holdout content (learner_state.replay rejects holdout attempts fail-closed, so no holdout item id can reach the journal this reads).

generated_at_day.description: The injected day counter — never a wall-clock timestamp (determinism).

open_count.description: Total open (unclosed) journal entries across all domains.

priority_count.description: Open entries flagged priority (an overconfident wrong answer).

domains.description: Ordered weakest-accuracy-first among SCORED domains; unscored domains (accuracy null — e.g. 'global'/exam_strategy, which learner_state.readiness_report excludes from per-domain accuracy, or 'unknown') sort last rather than being treated as 0% (review F1). Only domains with at least one open entry appear.

domain.description: 'unknown' is a schema-valid fallback for a journal entry whose item_id no longer resolves in the goldset (review F2) — never null.

trap.description: The registry's misconception text — the wrong belief.

priority.description: An overconfident wrong answer — the most dangerous kind of miss.

stem.description: The item's stem for context. Never its choices or any answer field.

closed_recap.description: Positive reinforcement: the latest occurrence of each recently-closed misconception (deduplicated), capped at 5.
